Petition For Writ Of Mandate To Review Decision Of Unemployment Insuarance Appeals Board
Off Calendar Dec-09-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER WAI MAN CHAN Petition For Writ Of Mandate To Review Decision Of Unemployment Insurance Appeals Board. Appearances: Wai Man Chan, Petitioner in Pro Per and moving party appearing; Hadara Stanton, Deputy Atty. Gen., Dept. of Justice, (415) 703-5561 appearing for Respondent, and opposing party. Anthony Musante, Esq. of Seyfarth Shaw LLP (415) 397-2823, representing Real Party In Interest HDR, Inc and CU H2A; Isabella Schwarvinger, President of AES, Real Party in Interest, (appearing, but not arguing). Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Petition denied. Petitioner fails to maintain her burden of proof. Petitioner does not show that respondent abused its discretion. The Administrative record was ordered returned to Hadara Stanton, Esq. in Open court. (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

Petition For Writ Of Mandate To Review Decision Of Unemployment Insuarance Appeals Board
Off Calendar Nov-09-2009 Continued To Dec-09-09 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER WAI CHAN Petition For Writ Of Mandate to Review Decision of Unemployment Insurance Appeals Board. Appearances: Wai Man Chan, Petitioner in Pro Per and moving party appearing; Hadara Stanton, Deputy Atty. Gen., Dept. of Justice, (415) 703-5561 appearing for Respondent. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Continued to December 9, 2009. Court strikes Plaintiff Exhibits 15a through 16f because they are not properly part of the administrative record. Respondent and real party in interest to file oppositions to the merits by November 30, 2009. Plaintiff's reply, points and authorities due by December 4, 2009. All parties to personally serve briefs and provide courtesy copies. (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

Ntc Of Hearing For Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Sep-04-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER WAI MAN CHAN Notice of Hearing for Writ of Mandate. Appearances: Petitioner in Pro Per Wai Man Chan appearing and Moving party; Hadara Stanton, Deputy Attorney General, Department of Justice, (415) 703-5561, appearing for Respondent and opposing party. Hearing held and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Petition for Writ denied without prejudice. Petitioner to re-calendar this matter after serving the real party in interest. In other words, petitioner needs to serve her former employer with this petition. (The Court retains the Administrative Record lodged by Respondent.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Response To Demand For Inspection And For Sanction And Demand For Inspection And Sanction
Off Calendar Aug-25-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Wai Man Chan, petitioner and moving party in Propria Persona
Hadara Stanton, Deputy Attorney General, for Defendant and opposing party: California Unemployment Insurance Appeals Board

After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted. Petitioner Wai Chan's Motion To Compel Further Response To Demand For Inspection And For Sanction denied. The Court previously has denied a substantially similar motion; the instant motion presents no new facts or law on which a different order might be based; nor does it establish that respondent is not in compliance.
Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Compliance In Accordance With Prior Response To Demand For Inspection And For Sanction
Off Calendar Aug-25-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Wai Man Chan, petitioner and moving party in Propria Persona
Hadara Stanton, Deputy Attorney General, for Defendant and opposing party: California Unemployment Insurance Appeals Board

After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted. Petitioner's Motion To Compel Compliance In Accordance With Prior Response To Demand For Inspection And For Sanction;
Motion to compel further response to demand for inspection and for sanction denied.
Parties shall meet and confer concerning the status and existence of a second case under a separate case number.
The Court previously has denied a substantially similar motion; the instant motion presents no new facts or law on which a different order might be based; nor does it establish that respondent is not in compliance. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.

No other appearance, tentative ruling is adopted. Petitioner Wai Chan's Motion to compel further response to demand for inspection and for sanction denied. Petition For Wriot Of Mandate
Off Calendar Apr-20-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER WAI CHAN'S Petition for Writ of Mandate. Appearances: Petitioner Pro Per, Wai Man Chan, present, (415) 699-4153. Petitioner failed to give notice, pursuant to San Francisco Local Rule 8.3. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Off Calendar. First, Plaintiff served his petition by mail and CCP section 1073 requires service in the same manner as a summons. Second, Plaintiff is seeking administrative mandamus under CCP section 1094.5 but has not lodged the transcript of the underlying hearing. (302KOPP)Judge: Quentin Kopp; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759
